name: Telomere protection and capping bootstrap pass for May 31, 2026

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Telomere Protection and Capping, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

summary: The track is coherent enough for a first public baseline at animal_signal. Human artery and endothelial-cell data support telomere uncapping as an age- and disease-relevant biomarker, but the direct intervention evidence remains preclinical, led by AAV9-TRF1 mouse healthspan data. The staged outlook keeps confidence low because shelterin biology is dose- and context-sensitive and no capping-directed human intervention with telomere-capping endpoints was identified.
notes: The most direct intervention signal is AAV9-TRF1 gene therapy in adult and old mice, which improved several healthspan markers and reduced short-telomere or telomere-associated DNA-damage signals in some tissues without significantly changing median telomere length.
Human artery studies support target relevance: telomere uncapping rises with age and hypertension and is linked to p53/p21 senescence signaling independently of mean telomere length.
Endothelial telomere dysfunction is causal in mice and associated with older human donor endothelial cells, but this still does not constitute human therapeutic evidence.

D+Q senolytics reduced endothelial telomere dysfunction-induced foci in old mice, but this is adjacent to the track and probably reflects senescent-cell clearance rather than direct telomere-capping repair.
TRF2 overexpression in mouse skin caused telomere loss, DNA damage, premature aging phenotypes, and increased cancer, so the forecast treats shelterin modulation as safety-sensitive.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity and interpretation/forecast evidence reviews before publication.
If approved, publish the staged source, study, finding, and outlook records as the first telomere protection and capping baseline.
After publication, inspect the telomere attrition hallmark page and track page for evidence ladder ordering and capping-versus-telomerase boundaries.
